title: Exporting All Subitems from Table Widget on a Dashboard
excerpt:
Hello, 
It appears that when you export the data in a table widget on a dashboard to Excel, it only exports the subitems that have been expanded manually in the widget. Is there any way to export all of the subitems, lik…

posts.author: Stu-Clearpath (Stu Moriens)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

It appears that when you export the data in a table widget on a dashboard to Excel, it only exports the subitems that have been expanded manually in the widget. Is there any way to export all of the subitems, like you can do when exporting a board to Excel? I am using a dashboard to bring many individual boards together into a single report. So, being able to export the dashboard to Excel and include all subitems would be extremely helpful.

Thanks!

posts.author: Morry (Takayuki Mori)
posts.cookedHtml: Voted and fully agreed, I need to export all the boards one by one to show the subtask level progress to customer.

posts.author: melissasd (Melissa Sutton-Dement)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there - having this feature would be very helpful for my work. Our workflows are based on subitems (the list would be unmanaagle if using only items), and we are working off 4 separate boards. We need any easy way to export all connected board subitems from a table widget. Right now, even when the subitems are expanded, it only exports our items which are basically categories in our workflow, and thus not informative.

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you are open to using an app, by using Master Data you connect all your Source boards to it, then can create various table views with filters on ‘Board Name’ in your Master Board. After that you can Group by Parent Item, or Board Name, and export from the Table view. The subitems will be synced as items on the Master Board. More info on the app can be found here: Master Data - Mint Consulting
